title: Bound a BigQuery query's cost before running it
description: Apply maximum_bytes_billed, partition filters, and a dry-run so an expensive query cannot silently scan terabytes.
body: Use when you are about to run or schedule a BigQuery query against a large table. Do not run an unbounded query against production data. Actions: run with --dry_run first to see bytes that would be billed, add a filter on the partition column, select only needed columns, and set maximum_bytes_billed so the job fails fast instead of scanning everything. Validation signals include the dry-run reporting bytes within budget and the job aborting if it would exceed the cap. Risk boundary: a query that the dry-run shows scanning beyond the agreed budget should be flagged to a human before running against production.
